title: Bug in : RTLIL::SigSpec::remove2 , when erasing bits the iterator "i" can point to erased bits.

features.name: nested generate blocks with multi-dimensional unpacked arrays
features.description: Code should include nested generate blocks iterating over multi-dimensional unpacked arrays where wire and reg arrays of different dimensions are interconnected
features.snippet:
wire [WIDTH-1:0] devices_data_out_p [DE-1:0];
generate for (i = 0; i < C_WIDTH; i=i+1) begin:gen_i for (j = 0; j < WIDTH; j=j+1) begin:gen_j assign devices_data_out_p[j][i] = devices_data_rotate[i][j]; end end endgenerate

features.name: unpacked array part-select with parameter multiplication
features.description: Code should include unpacked array assignments using part-select syntax with parameter-based width and offset calculations
features.snippet: for(client = 0; client < C_WIDTH; client = client + 1) begin clients_data_in[client] = c_data_in_port[client*WIDTH+:WIDTH]; end

features.name: 2D unpacked reg array with large dimension
features.description: Code should include a two-dimensional unpacked register array with dimension exceeding typical small values

features.name: reduction operator on unpacked array slice
features.description: Code should include reduction operator applied to a slice of unpacked array selected by index

features.name: complex signal routing through multiple array transformations
features.description: Code should include signal path that routes through multiple unpacked arrays via generate blocks with rotation and one-hot selection
features.snippet: wire [C_WIDTH-1:0] clients_data_rotate [WIDTH-1:0]; generate for (i = 0; i < C_WIDTH; i=i+1) begin:gen_i for (j = 0; j < WIDTH; j=j+1) begin:gen_j assign clients_data_rotate[j][i] = clients_data_in_s[i][j]; end end endgenerate
